summary: Critical blockers: (1) Test fixtures (simple-package.json, requirements.txt) referenced in workflow are never created—workflow will fail at 'Run license check' steps. (2) Check-DependencyLicenses.Tests.ps1 invoked but not provided. (3) actions/checkout@v4 not pinned to SHA—violates Actions security best practice; should use commit SHA with version comment. (4) Duplication in workflow lines 32–60: two near-identical blocks for package.json and requirements.txt should be factored into a helper script. (5) Misleading function names: Invoke-LicenseCheck only validates config structure, never actually checks licenses; real checking happens in New-ComplianceReport. (6) PowerShell missing Set-StrictMode and comprehensive error handling (-ErrorAction Stop). (7) Hardcoded mock-only testing with no real license lookup mechanism limits evolution. Would not run successfully.
